reason: Both findings are minor. Finding 1 (n:confirmationPage description) is low impact because the tag appears in hints and code examples, and the confirmation page pattern is inferable from context. Finding 2 (missing hints) is low impact because `abortIfInvalid` and `createObject` appear verbatim in the s1 section content — if the section is retrieved by any of the existing hints (ValidationContext, validateAndConvertRequest, etc.), the method names are present in the text. Hint omissions reduce retrieval optimization but do not constitute missing knowledge.

findings_assessment.location: s1 — JSP実装例（更新確認画面）
findings_assessment.justification: `n:confirmationPage` is already present in the hints array and in the code example. The missing prose explanation ('入力画面と同じ記述で確認項目の出力が出来る') would clarify the mechanism, but an agent reading the code example can infer the pattern. The absence does not cause incorrect answers, only slightly less explicit ones. Phase E did not fix this because the output JSON is identical to Phase D input — the fix agent made no changes.

`abortIfInvalid` and `createObject` appear verbatim in the s1 section body text (in the Java code block). If s1 is retrieved via any existing hint (ValidationContext, validateAndConvertRequest, W11AC03Action, etc.), both method names are present and the agent can answer questions about them. The missing hints reduce retrieval probability for method-name-specific queries, but do not constitute absent knowledge. Phase E did not fix this — the hints array in the Phase E output is identical to the Phase D state, indicating the fix agent silently skipped the change.
